ModMetadata.Name: Power Grid Plus
ModMetadata.Author: SixFive7
ModMetadata.Description:
[h1]Power Grid Plus[/h1]
Reworks Stationeers wired power: an atomic power tick with priority-based transformer dispatch, automatic shedding and overload lockouts, loop and producer-isolation faults with on-device countdowns, elastic battery allocation, three cable tiers as separate transmission voltages, logic-transparent transformer / battery / dish bridges, and deterministic multiplayer cable-network ids.
A pure-patch mod: no new craftable devices, no asset bundle. Parts of the simulation are derived from Sukasa's Re-Volt (MIT). Full multiplayer compatibility. Safe to remove from existing savegames: cable ratings are enforced at runtime and never written into the save.
This is an early build. The inner per-network simulation is the unmodified vanilla PowerTick; Power Grid Plus decides faults and allocations globally each tick and applies them the same tick, keeping vanilla partial power as the safety net for anything unclassified.
[h2]Features[/h2]
[h3]Faults: visible lockouts instead of silent misbehaviour[/h3] [list] [*][b]Shedding[/b] (orange flash): the input network cannot cover the device. Siblings compete by Priority; the lowest sheds first and its subnet goes dark cleanly for 60 seconds instead of flickering. [*][b]Overloaded[/b] (red flash): delivering everything Setting allows (or a battery at its full rate) while demand stays unmet. The bottleneck trips, so you know what to upgrade or split. [*][b]Cycle Fault[/b] (red flash): the device closes a power loop. Every loop member faults and contributes 0, dissolving the loop. No cable is burned for loops. [*][b]Variable Voltage Fault[/b] (red flash): a producer (solar, wind, generator, RTG) wired to consumers without a transformer stops generating. Producers may only share a network with producers and transformers. [*]Hover text names the cause with a live countdown. Toggling the device off clears the fault instantly; toggling back on re-evaluates. [/list]
[h3]Elastic storage[/h3] [list] [*]Batteries, APC cells, and rocket power umbilicals charge only from surplus and discharge only to fill a shortfall, sharing proportionally among competitors. No battery ping-pong. [*]Per-prefab charge / discharge rate caps, each also capped by the cable tier. [*]Read-only MaxChargeSpeed / MaxDischargeSpeed (configured caps) and ChargeSpeed / DischargeSpeed (live allocated rates) on every storage device. The earlier Import Quantity / Export Quantity exposure on batteries is removed. [*]Transformers no longer leak free power; APCs no longer leak power or drain their cell while idle. [/list]
[h3]Three-tier transmission voltages (new)[/h3] [list] [*]Cable Watts caps are configurable per tier (normal 5000, heavy 100000, super-heavy unlimited by default; 0 = unlimited) and enforced at runtime only -- cables in the save are never modified. [*]Only direct generator overflow burns cables. Transformer or battery overflow trips the upstream devices into Overloaded instead; the cable survives and the fault tells you what to fix. [*]Super-heavy cable costs twice as much to craft (configurable via the multiplier; 1.0 = vanilla). [*]The three cable tiers are three separate voltages. A cable network must be all one tier; a wrong-tier cable burns on the next powered tick (an idle network destroys nothing). The only legal bridge between tiers is a transformer or an APC. [*]Devices belong on the right tier: generators and stationary batteries on heavy, high-draw machines (the furnaces, Centrifuge, Recycler, and friends, plus any you add in the config) on heavy or normal, super-heavy is cables-and-transformers only, everything else on normal. A misplaced device burns the cable next to it when power flows (never the device itself). Transformers, APCs and wireless power devices are exempt. [*]Placement UX: placing a wrong-tier cable next to an existing device or network shows a red ghost with a "Wrong voltage" tooltip. Device placement is never blocked at the cursor. [*]Burned cables explain why in their hover tooltip ("Burned: overloaded", "Burned: wrong voltage"), and the reason survives save / load. [/list]
[h3]Transformer priority and dispatch (new)[/h3] [list] [*]The vanilla Setting value is the throughput cap again: IC10 reads and writes it exactly as in the base game. New transformers start at full throughput; saved worlds keep their saved Settings. [*]The in-world knob and the Labeller set [b]Priority[/b] instead (non-negative integer, no upper cap, default 100, step 10 per click or 1 with Alt). Priority decides who sheds first; comparisons are local to each input network. [*]New writable [b]Priority[/b] + read-only [b]Shedding[/b] / [b]Overloaded[/b] / [b]CycleFault[/b] logic values. [*]Priority saves with the world and rides the multiplayer join handshake. Master toggles for shedding and overload protection restore the vanilla behaviours when off. [*]Power transmitter / receiver dish pairs participate in dispatch like transformers, using whichever distance-loss model is active. [/list]
[h3]Logic passthrough (new)[/h3] [list] [*]Bridging devices (transformers, batteries, APCs, linked dish pairs) can be made logic-transparent: a reader sees through the bridge, and through a whole chain of bridges, with no extra data cable. Looped layouts are fine. [*]A writable LogicPassthroughMode logic value controls it per device (1 = transparent, 0 = vanilla); defaults to transparent for batteries, dishes and the small transformer. Saved with the world. Per-family server master toggles. [/list]
[h3]Settings[/h3] All settings are server-authoritative: in multiplayer the host's value controls gameplay for everyone. Highlights: [list] [*][b]Normal / Heavy / Super Heavy Cable Max Watts[/b] (5000 / 100000 / 0; 0 = unlimited), [b]Super-Heavy Cable Cost Multiplier[/b] (2.0). [*][b]Extra Heavy-Cable Devices[/b] (a comma-separated PrefabName list for modded high-draw machines). [*]Per-prefab battery charge / discharge caps, [b]Battery Charge Efficiency[/b], APC and Rocket Umbilical charge / discharge rates. [*][b]Enable Transformer Shedding[/b] and [b]Enable Transformer Overload Protection[/b] (both default true). [*][b]Enable Transformer / Battery / Power Transmitter / APC Logic Passthrough[/b] (all default true). [*][b]Emergency Light[/b] master toggle + a configurable prefab list for battery wall lights acting as emergency backup lights. [*]Voltage tiers, cycle faults, and producer isolation are always on (no toggle); they are the core of the redesigned grid. [/list]
[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[b]Requires:[/b] [list] [*][url=https://docs.bepinex.dev/]BepInEx[/url] [*][url=https://github.com/StationeersLaunchPad/StationeersLaunchPad]StationeersLaunchPad[/url] [/list]
[b]All players[/b] on a server must have Power Grid Plus installed; matching versions are enforced in the connection handshake. [b]Dedicated servers[/b] need the same setup server-side.
Re-Volt itself swaps the power tick the same way this mod does, so running both is not supported.
[h2]Reporting Issues[/h2] If you run into a bug or something behaves unexpectedly, please open an issue on [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/issues]GitHub[/url]. Please include the mod name in the title so reports can be triaged. Steam comment notifications don't always come through, so GitHub is the reliable way to make sure a report is seen.
[h2]Credits[/h2] [list] [*]Built on the power-simulation work in [url=https://github.com/sukasa/revolt]Re-Volt[/url] by Sukasa, used under the MIT License. The Re-Volt-derived portions retain their MIT notice; see the repository NOTICE. [/list]
[url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/tree/master/Mods/PowerGridPlus]Source Code on GitHub[/url]
[h2]License[/h2] Licensed under the [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/blob/master/LICENSE]Apache License 2.0[/url]. Attribution required; see the LICENSE and NOTICE files at the repository root.
ModMetadata.ChangeLog:
v0.2.0: Atomic power tick and the fault system (work in progress) - Atomic five-phase power tick: observe every network, decide every fault and allocation globally, then run the vanilla per-network power flow with those decisions in effect. Decisions apply in the same tick; the inner simulation is the unmodified vanilla PowerTick, and vanilla partial-power scaling stays as the safety net for unclassified devices. - Four 60-second fault lockouts with on-device feedback: Shedding (orange flash; input network cannot cover the device, lowest Priority sheds first), Overloaded (red; delivering at the Setting cap or full battery discharge with demand still unmet), Cycle Fault (red; the device closes a power loop, every loop member faults and the loop dissolves without burning a cable), and Variable Voltage Fault (red; a producer wired to consumers without a transformer stops generating). Hover text names the cause with a live countdown; toggling the device off clears the fault instantly. - Producer isolation is always on: producers (solar, wind, generators, RTG, turbine, dynamic generator dock) may only share a network with other producers and transformers. Unrecognised modded producers fall back to burning the cable next to the violating consumer. - Elastic storage: batteries, APC cells, and rocket power umbilicals charge only from surplus and discharge only to fill a shortfall, with proportional sharing among competitors. New read-only logic values MaxChargeSpeed, MaxDischargeSpeed, ChargeSpeed, and DischargeSpeed on all storage devices; the earlier Import Quantity / Export Quantity exposure on batteries is removed (breaking change for scripts that read them). - Transformer Setting is vanilla again: IC10 reads and writes the live throughput cap (the earlier redirect of Setting writes to Priority is removed); new transformers start at full throughput, saved worlds keep their saved Settings, and a Setting of 0 means deliberately off (logged at load). The knob and the Labeller set Priority (no upper cap). Dispatch is per input network by Priority. - Cable Watts caps are configurable per tier (normal 5000, heavy 100000 matching vanilla, super-heavy unlimited; 0 = unlimited) and enforced at runtime only; cables in the save are never modified, so removing the mod restores vanilla ratings. - Only direct generator overflow burns cables; transformer or battery overflow trips the upstream devices into Overloaded instead. Burned-cable reasons now survive save and load (side-car). - Power transmitter / receiver pairs participate in dispatch as transformer-like devices, using whichever distance-loss model is active (vanilla curve or PowerTransmitterPlus). - Rocket power umbilical rate caps and logic values (master toggle plus charge / discharge rate settings). - Multiplayer fault sync rebuilt as per-tick full snapshots with a join handshake, so clients always show the correct flash and countdown, self-healing after packet loss. - Emergency-light target list: the battery wall-light emergency behaviour now applies to a configurable prefab list (default StructureWallLightBattery). - Removed settings: Enable Voltage Tiers, Enable Recursive Network Limits, Enable Unlimited Super-Heavy Cables (voltage tiers and loop handling are always on; the super-heavy toggle became the 0 = unlimited cap). New settings: the three per-tier cable caps, APC Battery Discharge Rate, the three Rocket Umbilical entries, Enable Transformer Overload Protection, and Emergency Light Prefabs.
v0.1.0: First build (work in progress) - Reworked cable-network power tick from Re-Volt: proportional load sharing, gradual probabilistic cable burnout on a rolling throughput average, NaN-power guard. - Stationary battery charge/discharge-rate limits, optional charge efficiency, and rate-limit logic values. - Transformer free-power exploit fix, quiescent-draw restore, and Power Actual logic value. - Area Power Controller power-leak / idle-drain fix. - Recursive and looped networks allowed by default. - Super-heavy cable never burns; the three cable tiers are separate transmission voltages (mixing them burns the lower-tier boundary cable and splits the network); devices belong on their tier (generators and batteries on heavy, high-draw machines on heavy or normal, super-heavy is cables-and-transformers only, everything else on normal) and a device on the wrong tier gets no power; super-heavy cable coil costs 2x to craft (configurable via the multiplier). - Reactive tier enforcement: a wrong-tier cable burns on the next power tick if the network has actual power flow (an idle or off network destroys nothing). Wrong-tier cable placement is blocked at the cursor with a "Wrong voltage" tooltip; device placement is never blocked. Burned cables explain why they burned in their hover tooltip. - Logic passthrough: a writable LogicPassthroughMode logic value (set via IC10 or a logic writer) makes power-network bridging devices logic-transparent, so a logic reader sees devices on the far side, transitively across a whole chain of bridged devices (cycle-safe, so looped networks are fine). Covers transformers, stationary batteries, linked power transmitter and receiver dishes, and Area Power Controllers. The per-device mode is server-authoritative, persists across save and load, and each device family has its own master server toggle. - Transformer priority and load shedding: transformer throughput is hardcoded at the per-prefab OutputMaximum rating; the knob now sets a writable Priority value (default 100, step 10 per click or 1 with Alt). When transformers compete on the same input network, allocation is strictly by Priority: highest gets first dibs, leftover goes to the next priority. A transformer that cannot get its full share from the input network sheds for 10 seconds (contributes 0, on/off button pulses orange, hover shows "Shedding: insufficient upstream supply"); auto-clears after the hold. Two-tick tolerance prevents single-frame spikes from tripping a lockout. New writable Priority and read-only Shedding logic values; Setting writes are transparently redirected to Priority so legacy IC10 scripts keep working. Per-transformer Priority is saved with the world and synced across multiplayer in the join handshake. Server master toggle Enable Transformer Shedding (default on) restores vanilla behaviour when off. - Deterministic network merge: when cable or structure networks merge, the survivor is chosen by lowest reference id so the host and all clients agree on the same network id (a multiplayer correctness fix).
ModMetadata.InGameDescription: Power Grid Plus Reworks Stationeers wired power: an atomic power tick with priority-based transformer dispatch, shedding and overload lockouts, loop and producer-isolation faults with on-device countdowns, elastic battery allocation, three cable tiers as separate voltages, logic-transparent bridges, and deterministic multiplayer network ids. A pure-patch mod (no new prefabs). Multiplayer-compatible. Early build. Faults: - Shedding (orange flash), Overloaded / Cycle Fault / Variable Voltage Fault (red flash). 60 s lockouts, hover shows the cause with a live countdown, toggling off clears instantly. - Producers (solar, wind, generators) may only share a network with producers and transformers. Dispatch: Setting is the vanilla throughput cap; the knob and Labeller set Priority. Lowest priority sheds first per input network. Loops fault their devices instead of burning cables. Storage: batteries / APCs / umbilicals charge only from surplus, discharge only to fill shortfalls. MaxChargeSpeed / MaxDischargeSpeed / ChargeSpeed / DischargeSpeed logic values. Tiers: three cable tiers are separate voltages; configurable Watts caps, runtime-enforced (saves untouched). Only generator overflow burns cables. Requires: BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad.
